meals.label: Meal A
meals.name: Healthy Chicken Pot Pie
ings: 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper
1 cup unsweetened almond milk (or low-fat milk)
3 cups cooked shredded chicken (rotisserie works great)
½ cup frozen pearl onions (or ½ small onion, diced and sautéed)
1 tsp fresh thyme (or ½ tsp dried)
1 store-bought deep dish pie crust (Great Value works — use as top crust only)
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Lightly grease a 9-inch pie dish or use a cast iron skillet.

Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or deep skillet over medium-high. Add mushrooms and cook 8 min until browned and sweated. Add carrots, celery, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook 3 more min until carrots begin to soften.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well. Cook 2 minutes so the flour loses its raw taste.
Pour in chicken broth slowly, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Add almond milk. Cook over medium heat, stirring regularly, until sauce thickens — about 5–6 minutes. Do not boil hard.
Stir in shredded chicken, frozen peas, pearl onions, and thyme. Taste and adjust salt. Remove from heat.
Transfer filling to your prepared pie dish (or leave in cast iron if oven-safe).
Unroll the pie crust from the package. Lay it over the top of the dish, trimming to leave about ½ inch overhang. Press edges to seal and crimp with a fork. Cut 3–4 small slits to vent steam.
Brush crust all over with beaten egg wash.
Bake 20–25 minutes until crust is deeply golden and filling is bubbling at the edges. Let rest 5–10 minutes before serving — the filling will thicken as it sits.
meals.note: 💡 Top-crust only keeps this lighter and the crust stays flaky (no soggy bottom). Rotisserie chicken is the easy shortcut here. The Great Value deep dish crust works perfectly as a top-only crust — just unroll it over the dish.

meals.label: Meal B
meals.name: Creamy Chicken Tortilla Soup
ings: 1 (10 oz) can Rotel diced tomatoes with green chilies (mild)
1 (14 oz) can black beans, drained & rinsed
Tortilla strips, avocado, sour cream, Tex-Mex cheese for topping
steps: Sauté onion in oil over medium-high 5–7 min until lightly browned.
Stir in tomatoes, Rotel, broth, spices, corn, and beans. Increase to high.
Boil, then reduce and simmer lid slightly ajar for 5 min.
Stir in cream and chicken. Warm through 3–5 min. Season and serve with toppings.
meals.note: 💡 Use the mild Rotel (green label) and skip any extra heat — the soup is plenty flavorful for the kids. Leftovers from Monday's pot pie chicken work great here.

meals.label: Meal C
meals.name: Creamy Spinach and Mushroom Tortellini
ings: ¼ tsp salt, ¼ tsp black pepper
steps: Caramelize onions: heat 2 Tbsp oil over medium-high; cook onions 10 min stirring constantly. Reduce to medium, cook 10 more min. Add pinch of salt, cook 10 min on medium-low. Total: 30 min. Remove to a plate.
Heat 1 Tbsp oil in same skillet over medium. Cook mushrooms with a pinch of salt 5 min until soft.
Add spinach; stir on low until wilted. Add back caramelized onions. Pour in cream and salt; bring to quick boil. Reduce, simmer 2 min.
Cook tortellini per package; drain. Add to sauce; stir on low a few minutes. Serve with cracked black pepper.
meals.note: 💡 Caramelized onions can be made 1–2 days ahead and refrigerated — do it Monday while the pot pie bakes to save time Wednesday.
